overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a highly active and engaged professional presence across decentralized and professional social networks, specifically Bluesky, Mastodon, and LinkedIn. His content consistently focuses on computational social science, disinformation, coordinated inauthentic behavior, and academic career opportunities. Across all platforms, he commands a total audience of 3,580 followers, achieving high qualitative engagement particularly when sharing research outputs, software releases, and recruitment opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Updates on software tools (CooRnet) and newly published research analyzing coordinated link-sharing and computational methods.
Job openings, postdoctoral roles, and PhD opportunities within his research team, which drive high interactions.
High-profile announcements regarding newly awarded research grants and upcoming conference presentations like DisinfoConf.

recommendations: Capitalize on Bluesky's high engagement rate by prioritizing it for time-sensitive academic calls and paper announcements.
Utilize LinkedIn's larger reach exclusively for professional milestones, funding news, and recruitment to maximize applicant pools.
Diversify Mastodon content to focus on the technical details and open-source aspects of tools like CooRnet, appealing to its developer-friendly audience.

platformInsights.bluesky: Yields the highest overall engagement rate (averaging over 6.1%), with audiences showing exceptional responsiveness to publication announcements and PhD opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Serves as a robust secondary platform with an active technical audience, proving highly receptive to tool updates like CooRnet v2.0.
platformInsights.linkedin: Represents the largest raw follower base, generating the highest absolute engagement numbers (averaging 90 interactions per post) particularly for funding success and postdoctoral hiring.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
